languages.name: English
languages.bornNative: Happy Birthday — the day you were born

stateLang.state: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu

stateFormation.from: Andhra State (carved out of Madras State 1-Oct-1953) merged with the Telugu districts of Hyderabad State; born in coastal Andhra before Oct 1953 = born in Madras State. Lost Telangana on 2-Jun-2014
was NEFA (North-East Frontier Agency), administered as part of Assam until 1972, then a Union Territory
Assam Province of British India; since gave away Nagaland (1963), Meghalaya (1972), Mizoram and Arunachal (1972 as UTs)
Bihar Province; its entire southern half became Jharkhand on 15-Nov-2000
carved out of Madhya Pradesh
Portuguese territory until 19-Dec-1961, then part of the UT of Goa, Daman and Diu; born in Goa before Dec 1961 = born under Portuguese rule
carved out of bilingual Bombay State; born in Ahmedabad before May 1960 = born in Bombay State
carved out of Punjab
a Union Territory (earlier Part C state of merged hill princely states); gained Kangra and other hill areas of Punjab on 1-Nov-1966
carved out of Bihar
unified Mysore State — old Mysore princely state plus Kannada areas of Bombay, Hyderabad and Madras States and Coorg
merger of Travancore-Cochin with the Malabar district of Madras State; born in Kozhikode before Nov 1956 = born in Madras State
merger of old Madhya Pradesh (ex Central Provinces and Berar), Madhya Bharat, Vindhya Pradesh and Bhopal State; lost Chhattisgarh on 1-Nov-2000
carved out of bilingual Bombay State; born in Bombay before May 1960 = born in Bombay State
princely kingdom until Oct 1949, then a Part C state / Union Territory
carved out of Assam (autonomous state within Assam from 2-Apr-1970); born in Shillong before 1972 = born in Assam's capital
was the Lushai Hills / Mizo Hills district of Assam until 1972, then a Union Territory
carved out of Assam (Naga Hills district and Tuensang Area)
Orissa Province of British India; spelling anglicised as Orissa for 61 years of the republic
trifurcation of the larger East Punjab (which had absorbed PEPSU in 1956) — Haryana split off, hill areas went to Himachal, Chandigarh became a UT
union of the Rajputana princely states (integrated 1949); took current boundaries when Ajmer State merged in on 1-Nov-1956
independent Himalayan kingdom under the Chogyal (an Indian protectorate); born in Gangtok before May 1975 = not born in India at all
Madras Presidency became Madras State; Telugu districts left for Andhra State on 1-Oct-1953, Malabar left and Kanyakumari joined on 1-Nov-1956
carved out of Andhra Pradesh; anyone born in Hyderabad 1956–2014 was born in Andhra Pradesh — and before 1-Nov-1956, in Hyderabad State
princely kingdom until Oct 1949, then a Part C state / Union Territory
was the United Provinces of British India, renamed two days before the republic; lost its hill districts to Uttarakhand on 9-Nov-2000
carved out of Uttar Pradesh as Uttaranchal
the Indian half of partitioned Bengal Province; absorbed Cooch Behar (1950) and French Chandernagore (1954)
a Part D territory of the new republic, made a Union Territory by the States Reorganisation Act
carved out of Punjab as a Union Territory to serve as shared capital of Punjab and Haryana

stateFormation.state: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
stateFormation.from: merger of two UTs — Dadra & Nagar Haveli (freed from Portugal 1954, UT from 11-Aug-1961) and Daman & Diu (Portuguese until Dec 1961, part of Goa UT until 30-May-1987)
Part C state until 1956, then a plain Union Territory; became the National Capital Territory with its own assembly under the 69th Amendment
was a full state with special status under Article 370 for the entire 1950–2010 window; downgraded to a Union Territory and split from Ladakh
was the Leh and Kargil districts of Jammu and Kashmir state; anyone born there 1950–2010 was born in J&K
detached from Madras State as the UT of Laccadive, Minicoy and Amindivi Islands
French India — de facto handover 1-Nov-1954, de jure only on 16-Aug-1962; born there before Nov 1954 = born in France's India

ta.note: Sivaji Ganesan's thunderous debut — Dravidian cinema finds its voice
The comedy every Tamil household can still quote
MGR directs MGR — a phenomenon, not just a film
Sivaji's roaring freedom-fighter epic
Brother-sister tearjerker; Tamil cinema at its most beloved
The colour comedy that never aged
MGR's double role ruled the year
Sivaji and Padmini — dance versus nadaswaram
MGR circles the globe; box-office records fall
A young Rajinikanth walks through the gate — cinema history
Bharathiraja takes Tamil film out of the studio into the village
Rajinikanth's first great performance
Rajini remakes Don and becomes a superstar for good
Kamal's heartbreak classic — that final scene at the level crossing
Mani Ratnam arrives; the modern Tamil romance is born
Kamal's godfather — in TIME's all-time 100 films
Kamal as Appu — every child of 1989 remembers
Rajini and Mani Ratnam, Karna retold in the slums
A. R. Rahman erupts; India gets a new sound
Auto-driver with a past — the definitive Rajini mass film
Kamal doubles as freedom fighter and vigilante
'En vazhi thani vazhi' — Rajini at full wattage
Mani Ratnam's marriage story; Rahman's spring-water score
Vijay sprints into superstardom
Rajini versus the haunted room; a year-long run
Shankar + Rajini — the costliest Indian film of its day
Kamal Haasan plays ten roles
Rajini the robot — Indian cinema goes sci-fi blockbuster

ml.note: The film that put Malayalam cinema on the national map
Kerala's sea-spray classic — first South film to win the President's Gold Medal
Mohanlal and Sreenivasan — the unemployment comedy Kerala never stopped quoting
Ran 366 days straight — a Kerala record
Mohanlal's broken-dream policeman's son; a generation wept
Feuding families, endless laughs — ran over a year
The psychological classic remade by every other industry
Mohanlal mass mode; Mollywood learns the word blockbuster
The campus reunion that started the nostalgia wave

te.note: NTR and the sorcerer — fantasy filmmaking decades ahead
NTR, ANR and Savitri in one frame
Voted India's greatest film ever in a CNN-IBN poll — Ghatotkacha's feast
First Telugu colour film; mythological box-office thunder
A classical-music film that outran every mass entertainer
Chiranjeevi breaks out — the angry young Telugu decade begins
Chiranjeevi and a goddess on earth — pure magic
First Telugu film to gross ₹10 crore; Chiranjeevi is Megastar
Chiranjeevi thunder at the peak of mass cinema
A young Rajamouli and Jr NTR announce themselves
Rajamouli reincarnation epic — the road to Baahubali starts here

kn.note: Dr. Rajkumar debuts — Kannada cinema finds its Annavru
Rajkumar's farmer epic ran two straight years
Rajkumar as the Kadamba king — pride of Karnataka
Upendra's cult film — re-released more times than anyone can count
The monsoon romance that ran a full year and revived Sandalwood

bn.note: Satyajit Ray's debut — Cannes bows to Apu and Durga
Ray's own favourite of all his films
Uttam Kumar plays a matinee idol for Ray — meta before meta
Ray's singing-ghost fantasy every Bengali child knows by heart
Feluda rides to Jaisalmer — detective fiction on golden sand
'Dori dhore maro taan' — satire Bengal still quotes at politicians

eras.headline: Shyamchi Aai and the sant tradition
eras.story: Shyamchi Aai (1953) won India's first-ever National Film Award; Marathi cinema was the conscience of the young republic.

eras.headline: Pinjra and the Dada Kondke decade
eras.story: V. Shantaram's Pinjra (1972) and Dada Kondke's record run of silver-jubilee comedies packed Maharashtra's single screens.

eras.headline: Shwaas revives Marathi cinema
eras.story: Shwaas (2004) went to the Oscars as India's official entry and lit the fuse of the Marathi new wave.

eras.headline: Nanak Naam Jahaz Hai
eras.story: The 1969 devotional landmark — Punjab queued for months; a National Award followed.

eras.headline: Chann Pardesi
eras.story: The 1981 National Award winner anchored Punjabi cinema through a difficult decade.

eras.headline: Gujarati cinema's golden run
eras.story: Jesal Toral (1971) kicked off the boom years — mythologicals and folk tales filled Gujarat theatres.

eras.headline: The Bhupen Hazarika era
eras.story: Assam's cinema grew around Dr. Bhupen Hazarika's voice and films — Shakuntala (1961) won national honours.
